title: Osvald Teshik
content:

{{Quote|Nobody believes in their own end until it's upon them, until it can't be escaped.|Grand Admiral Teshik|<ref name="EGW" />}}
'''Osvald Teshik''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] who served as a [[Grand Admiral/Legends|Grand Admiral]] in the [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy]]. A respected strategist, he was one of the original twelve individuals to be appointed to the [[Military rank/Legends|rank]] of Grand Admiral by [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] [[2 BBY/Legends|two years before]] the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]]. In this position, Teshik commanded the [[Imperial Center Oversector]], a vast region of [[Realspace/Legends|space]] that included the [[The galaxy/Legends|galactic]] [[Capital/Legends|capital]] of [[Coruscant/Legends|Imperial Center]] and all of the [[Oversector/Legends|oversector's]] [[Fleet/Legends|fleets]].

In [[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]], Teshik was tasked with recovering [[Imperial Advisor/Legends|Imperial Advisor]] [[Coh Veshiv]] after the advisor was captured by the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]] [[Privateer/Legends|privateer]] [[EF76 Nebulon-B escort frigate/Legends|frigate]] ''[[Far Orbit]]''. He failed to rescue Veshiv or deal with the vessel's [[Dhas Fenoep Vedij|renegade captain]], prompting Palpatine to send him into the [[Hapes Cluster/Legends|Hapes Cluster]] to [[Battle of Andalia|engage]] the [[Hapan Royal Navy]] with insufficient firepower and supplies. Nearly killed in the battle, the Grand Admiral was left to drift in [[Realspace/Legends|space]] until he was retrieved by an Imperial [[snoopship]]. As a result of injuries he sustained during the engagement, seventy-five percent of his body had to be replaced with [[Cybernetics/Legends|cybernetic parts]]. Teshik was shunned by many as a result, who saw him as a weak and repulsive [[Cyborg/Legends|cyborg]].

[[4 ABY/Legends|Four years after]] the incident at Andalia, Teshik was present aboard the [[Death Star II/Legends|second Death Star]] during the Rebel Alliance's [[Battle of Endor/Legends|attack]] on the [[Battle station/Legends|battle station]]. When the tide of the battle turned against the Empire, he narrowly escaped the [[Death Star/Legends|Death Star]] before its destruction to continue the fight against the Alliance from his [[Flagship/Legends|flagship]], the [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer]] ''[[Eleemosynary]]''. For four hours after the remnants of the Imperial fleet fled into [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]], Teshik pursued battle with the Rebels until his Star Destroyer was disabled by concentrated [[Ion cannon/Legends|ion cannon]] fire. Captured by the Alliance and sentenced to [[Death/Legends|death]] for committing crimes against galactic citizens, Teshik mocked his captors with mechanical laughter, warning them that their fight was far from over.

==Biography==
===Grand Admiral===
{{Quote|My men thought we were a recon patrol and that our mission was to keep the Hapans honest. But I knew better—I knew we were going to a place from which we'd never return.|Grand Admiral Teshik, reflecting on the Battle of Andalia|<ref name="EGW" />}}
[[File:Anaxes.jpg|thumb|left|Elements of Teshik's forces participate in a parade over Anaxes.]]
Osvald Teshik was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] who lived during the reign of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]].<ref name="Who's Who" /> Though generations of his family resided in the [[Sirpar Hills]] of the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Anaxes/Legends|Anaxes]], Teshik spent summers on the planet [[Kallistas]] when he was young, staring at the [[Star/Legends|stars]] in the night sky. In later [[Standard year/Legends|years]], he served as an [[Admiral/Legends|admiral]] in the [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy]], and it was through this service that he earned the reputation of being a renowned strategist.<ref name="EGW" /> In [[2 BBY/Legends|2 BBY]], Teshik was elevated to the newly created [[Military rank/Legends|rank]] of [[Grand Admiral/Legends|Grand Admiral]] by [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]]. As one of the twelve Grand Admirals, he answered directly to the [[Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] and served as a leader of the [[Imperial Military/Legends|Imperial Military]]. Teshik was one of Palpatine's most important and trusted Grand Admirals, as he commanded [[Imperial Center Oversector]], which embraced the very [[Coruscant/Legends|capital]] of the Empire and the government's most developed, wealthy worlds,<ref name="Who's Who" /> such as [[Anaxes/Legends|Anaxes]]. This position put him in command of the various [[Fleet/Legends|fleets]] that were attached to [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy's]] most important [[Oversector/Legends|oversector]], one of which was the [[First Army|Azure Hammer Command]], which included the [[Executor-class Star Dreadnought/Legends|''Executor''-class Star Dreadnought]] ''[[Whelm]]'' and fifty-seven other [[Capital ship/Legends|capital ships]].<ref name="Coruscant and the Core Worlds">''[[Coruscant and the Core Worlds]]''</ref> Teshik took his duty to keep the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]] safe very seriously,<ref name="Who's Who" /> but Azure Hammer Command and the [[Anaxes Citadel]], the center of government on the planet of the same name, only answered to him until the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]].<ref name="CSWE">''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]''</ref>

In [[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]], [[Imperial Advisor/Legends|Imperial Advisor]] [[Coh Veshiv]] was abducted from a [[Space station/Legends|space station]] above [[Esseles/Legends|Esseles]] by the [[Privateer/Legends|privateer]] [[EF76 Nebulon-B escort frigate/Legends|Nebulon-B frigate]] ''[[Far Orbit]]''. Because Esseles fell within Teshik's domain, Palpatine tasked the Grand Admiral with retrieving Veshiv. As an added incentive, the Emperor personally threatened Teshik with execution if he did not immediately deal with the [[Starship/Legends|ship]] and its [[Captain/Legends|captain]], [[Dhas Fenoep Vedij]].<ref name="Far Orbit">''[[The Far Orbit Project]]''</ref> When Teshik failed to rescue Veshiv and kill Vedij, Palpatine ordered the Grand Admiral to attack the isolationist [[Hapes Consortium/Legends|Hapes Consortium]] with insufficient forces and matériel as an example to the other Grand Admirals that even they were not beyond reproach.<ref name="Who's Who" /> Teshik was placed in command of a [[Pursuit-class light cruiser|''Pursuit''-class light cruiser]], the ''[[Shepherd]]'', and a small fleet comprised of eight [[Gunship/Legends|gunships]] and two [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE fighter]] [[Squadron/Legends|squadrons]] before being dispatched to Consortium space.<ref name="EGW" />

Upon arrival above the planet [[Hapes/Legends|Hapes]], Teshik received orders to proceed to Andalia, where he was to [[Battle of Andalia|attack]] the [[Hapan Royal Navy|Hapan fleet]]. The [[Hapan Battle Dragon]]s and [[Nova-class battle cruiser|''Nova''-class battle cruisers]] present at Andalia ripped Teshik's forces apart with ease, and once the ''Shepherd'' was damaged, the Grand Admiral sealed himself in a [[Environment suit/Legends|vac suit]] before being sucked out into space through a hull breach. Shortly after regaining consciousness while floating in the void, he experienced a vision, which—as he later claimed—showed him the past and the future of the galaxy. Eventually, he was picked up by an Imperial [[snoopship]] sent there so the Emperor could observe Teshik's doomed mission.<ref name="EGW" /> Teshik had not escaped unhurt, however, and seventy-five percent of his body had to be replaced with [[Cybernetics/Legends|cybernetic parts]]. Though he retained his position of Grand Admiral, Teshik became the target of much abuse and discrimination due to his extensive cybernetics. As a result, the Grand Admiral completely cut himself off from his emotions.<ref name="Who's Who" />

===Battle of Endor and death===
{{Quote|Go ahead, Rebel—let's get it over with. Turn Grand Admiral Teshik to smoke. But remember what I saw and take heed of what I said. You'll be smoke, too, soon enough. For each of our wars is just one little piece of a greater war, one endless and incalculably larger.|Teshik, to his Rebel captors after the Battle of Endor|<ref name="EGW" />}}
[[File:BattleOfAndalia-EGW.jpg|thumb|right|250px|At Andalia, Teshik barely survived an encounter with the Hapan navy.]]
[[4 ABY/Legends|Four years later]], alongside Grand Admirals [[Nial Declann]], [[Miltin Takel]], and [[Afsheen Makati]], Teshik accompanied Emperor Palpatine to the [[Death Star II/Legends|second Death Star]] as the [[Battle station/Legends|battle station]] was being constructed above the [[Endor/Legends|Forest Moon of Endor]].<ref name="Who's Who" /> The [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]], learning of Palpatine's presence aboard the new [[Death Star/Legends|Death Star]], launched an attack against the station, believing the Imperial fleet to be scattered across the galaxy. However, it was a ruse orchestrated by the Emperor, and the Rebels had fallen into the trap. Under the command of [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Firmus Piett/Legends|Firmus Piett]] aboard the [[Star Dreadnought/Legends|Star Dreadnought]] [[Executor/Legends|''Executor'']], the Imperial fleet engaged the Rebels in an effort to annihilate them once and for all.<ref name="ROTJ">{{Film|VI}}</ref> However, when the ambush backfired and the Death Star became critically damaged, Teshik attempted to escape the station, only to be pinned beneath a fallen column. Of the myriad of Imperials fleeing the station, only one—a construction worker—stopped to free him. The worker's generosity and kindness moved Teshik, who regained the long-lost compassion he had once shown toward others.<ref name="Who's Who" />

Escaping the station, Teshik arrived aboard his personal [[Command ship/Legends|command ship]], the [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer]] ''[[Eleemosynary]]''. With the Emperor, [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]], and Grand Admiral Declann dead, and Grand Admirals Takel and Makati fleeing, Teshik assumed command of the remaining Imperial forces and prosecuted the fight against the [[Alliance Fleet/Legends|Alliance Fleet]]. He fought off the Rebels on his own after the remnants of the Imperial fleet fled under [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Gilad Pellaeon/Legends|Gilad Pellaeon]], commander of the Star Destroyer [[Chimaera/Legends|''Chimaera'']]. It took four hours of intense fighting before Teshik's [[Flagship/Legends|flagship]] was finally disabled by a barrage of [[Ion cannon/Legends|ion cannon]] fire. The Alliance captured Teshik alive, put him on trial for "inhuman atrocities committed against the citizens of the galaxy," convicted him, and sentenced him to [[Death/Legends|death]]. The irony of the sentencing did not escape Teshik, and before his execution, he responded with mechanical laughter.<ref name="Who's Who" /> In his final statement, he relayed the story of his experiences at Andalia, warning the Rebels that their fight with the Empire was far from over.<ref name="EGW" /> Teshik became one of the few Imperials executed for war crimes by the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]], the Alliance's successor government.<ref name="Who's Who" />

==Personality and traits==
{{Quote|Hutt and Tionese, Republic and Sith, Loyalist and Separatist, Imperial and Rebel—I knew it didn't matter who had killed whom, or when or how or why. All at once I understood that those of us born to be sacrificed upon the pyre of war become one when we die—mingled smoke gone up to whatever gods you believe in. For they are the ones who created war. And they breathed it into our hearts when they created us.|Teshik, reflecting on his vision above Andalia|<ref name="EGW" />}}
Osvald Teshik was notable for his confidence and compassion, something very rare among high Imperial officials. He was dedicated to the Imperial cause, taking his position as commander of Imperial Center Oversector seriously, and he had a keen mind for military strategy. As a testament to his ability, Teshik was able to stand his ground against the enemy even when they outnumbered him. He refused to surrender even when the odds were against him, such as in his engagements with the Hapan navy in the Hapes Cluster and the Alliance Fleet at Endor. In both situations his losses were personal: he lost seventy-five percent of his organic body in the skirmish with the Hapan fleet, and he persevered when he led the remnants of the Imperial fleet at Endor even when faced with utter hopelessness.<ref name="Who's Who" /> At times, his Academy training would unconsciously kick in.<ref name="EGW" />

During his near-death experience at Andalia, Teshik experienced a vision while floating in the void, a vision he later relayed to his Rebel captors. Over the course of the experience, the Grand Admiral saw "[[Celestial/Legends|beings made of light]]" and several battles from both the distant past and future, images that convinced him that all warriors merge into one otherworldly collective upon death. In the epiphany, Teshik concluded that conflict between beings was unavoidable, and that individual struggles were merely small parts of a much larger, all-encompassing war.<ref name="EGW" />

He was also known for the cybernetic replacements he received following his encounter with the Hapan navy. He had a [[Color/Legends|red]] prosthetic right eye, numerous wires entering both his skull and throat, and metal plates bolted to his forehead. However, because the Empire discriminated against the ill and weak—serious mutilations were rare due to widespread use of [[Bacta/Legends|bacta]]—Teshik was universally shunned and an object of revulsion. Being ostracized, the shame and unending prejudice he experienced hardened his [[Heart/Legends|heart]]. As a result, he lost his compassion, and it was not until he was freed from the fallen column aboard the second Death Star that he regained this.<ref name="Who's Who" /> He also admitted he wasn't sure why Palpatine was Emperor prior to his execution, citing that "being Emperor was its own why" when asked why he nearly had him executed, and reiterated this statement regarding why Palpatine bothered to save him.<ref name="EGW" />

==Behind the scenes==
{{Quote|Teshik's execution was nothing necessarily just, just politics as usual. Someone prominent had to take the fall for the atrocities committed by the Empire, but with Vader and Palp both already dead, only the GA in charge of the Core and that held off the Rebels for hours after the Death Star's destruction made a suitable showstopper. Tragically, Teshik was perhaps the least 'evil' of the GAs. Life's tough.|Abel G. Peña|<ref name="sw.com">{{SWBoards|url=thread.jspa?threadID=169992&start=132|thread=The other Imperial Grand Admirals…|user=[[Abel G. Peña|Halagad]]|date=2005-01-26|time=2:46 PM}}</ref>}}
Osvald Teshik was first mentioned in [[1998]]'s ''[[The Far Orbit Project]]'', written by [[Timothy S. O'Brien]] and published by [[West End Games]].<ref name="Far Orbit" /> It was not until [[2003]]'s ''[[Who's Who: Imperial Grand Admirals]]'', by [[Abel G. Peña]] and [[Daniel Wallace]], that Teshik was given a backstory.<ref name="Who's Who" /> Along with Captain [[Xamuel Lennox]] of the [[Rebel Dream|''Tyrant'']] and [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]] [[TIE pilot]] [[Maarek Stele/Legends|Maarek Stele]], Abel G. Peña considers Teshik to be one of his "good" Imperials, "making the best of ethically impossible circumstances."<ref name="Peña email">{{EmailCite|email=Wookieepedia:Email/Abel G. Peña|creator=[[Abel G. Peña]]|date=2008-04-28}}</ref> Peña has also said that Teshik's story was intended to be a tragic one.<ref name="sw.com" /> Teshik received an entry in [[2008]]'s ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]''.

Before appearing in [[Star Wars Insider 66|''Star Wars Insider'' 66]], the article ''Who's Who: Imperial Grand Admirals'' was originally planned to appear in an issue of ''[[Star Wars Gamer]]'' under the name "''[[Dirty Dozen: The Imperial Grand Admirals]]''" until the magazine was discontinued.<ref name="Blog">{{Blog|danwallace|16|Grand Admirals: What Have They Got in their Pocketsses?}}</ref> There are few differences between the biography present in ''Dirty Dozen'' and the final one present in ''Who's Who''; for example, while the initial version of the article established his flagship, the ''Eleemosynary'', as an [[Imperial I-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperial''-class Star Destroyer]], ''Who's Who'' eliminated any mention of its class. Three additional pieces of information that did not make it into the final article are described in the accompanying section below.<ref name="Who's Who" /><ref name="cut">"[[Dirty Dozen: The Imperial Grand Admirals]]"</ref>

{{Cutstart}}
===Cut content===
Teshik never had reservations about the Empire. When sent to eliminate the crew of the ''Far Orbit'', he was also tasked with securing the [[Hyperspace route/Legends|spacelanes]] from Vedij's plundering of the [[Ringali Shell]], home to half a dozen highly-populated [[Star system/Legends|star systems]]. If he failed, Palpatine threatened to behead the Grand Admiral. Following the engagement with the Hapan fleet and his reconstruction as a [[Cyborg/Legends|cyborg]] with a built-in [[Blaster cannon/Legends|blaster cannon]], Teshik did not become a [[Droid/Legends|droid]]-hater like [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtrooper]]-turned-[[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Beilert Valance/Legends|Beilert Valance]] or end up infuriated with organics like the largely-cybernetic recluse [[Kligson/Legends|Kligson]]. At the time of his execution, his artificial, hollow laughter was the saddest sound the Rebels present ever heard.<ref name="cut" />
{{Cutend}}

===Inconsistencies===
There is an issue over when the Imperial retreat was called during the Battle of Endor. ''Who's Who'' states that the retreat was ordered hours before Teshik was captured by the Alliance,<ref name="Who's Who" /> but ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' says that Captain Gilad Pellaeon called the retreat after Teshik's flagship was disabled.<ref name="NEC">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''</ref> ''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]'' reaffirms the former, stating that Pellaeon and the fleet fled four hours before Teshik was captured.<ref name="EGW" />

While ''The Essential Guide to Warfare'' clarifies the timing of the Imperial retreat, it introduces a slight inconsistency regarding the Grand Admiral's homeworld. In the book's prologue, which consists of Teshik's final words, he states that he spent time on Kallistas as a youth, hinting that he is from the planet. However, later in the book, it is implied that Teshiks had resided on Anaxes for generations. This article has attempted to reconcile these two pieces of information.<ref name="EGW" />
